title: Wave3 G2+G5:重装误报与折叠死结(detail 按 r.changed + 折叠跨 flushed buffer)+ spinner 单行原地刷新
description: Fix G2 and G5 in pipeline.ts + ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts. G2 has two roots: (a) buildSummary's per-stage detail uses r.installed.length so a no-change re-ensure misreports '3 installed' — switch the detail to say '已最新/up-to-date' when r.changed!==true; (b) a mid-pipeline clack prompt calls buffer.flushTo → buffer.flushed=true, which bypasses the collapse gate `if (buffer && !buffer.flushed && allIdempotent)` — make the collapse fire on all-idempotent runs even when the buffer was flushed by a prompt. G5: renderStep emits a 'running' line then a 'success' line, both persisting (double line) — redraw in place on TTY with `\x1b[1A\x1b[2K`; non-TTY prints ONLY the final line (no running placeholder).
action: pipeline.ts buildSummary (~line 400): change the `value` for a ran stage from `${r.installed.length} installed` to a t() that branches on r.changed — `r.changed === true ? t('cli.install.stage.installed-count',{count}) : t('cli.install.stage.uptodate')`. Collapse gate (~line 316): change condition so an all-idempotent run collapses regardless of buffer.flushed, e.g. `if (buffer !== undefined && this.allIdempotent(context)) { ... renderSummaryCard(buildHealthCheckSummary...) }` and ensure the normal-path flush below is skipped on that branch (guard double-emit since a flushed buffer already replayed). renderStep in ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts: track whether the previous emitted line for this step was a 'running' placeholder; when colorOn && process.stdout.isTTY and the new status is terminal (success/skipped/error) for the same step, write `\x1b[1A\x1b[2K` before the final line to overwrite the running line in place; when non-TTY, suppress the 'running' emit entirely and print only the terminal line.

criteria: pipeline.ts buildSummary detail status word is derived from r.changed (grep 'changed' inside buildSummary), NOT r.installed.length — grep confirms the ran-stage `value` no longer reads `installed.length`
en.ts AND zh-CN.ts both define 'cli.install.stage.uptodate' and 'cli.install.stage.installed-count' (grep both keys in both files)

pipeline.ts collapse gate no longer requires `!buffer.flushed` to fire on an all-idempotent run (grep the collapse `if` — `!buffer.flushed` is removed from the all-idempotent collapse condition) and the all-idempotent branch does not double-emit a flushed buffer
ConsoleOutputRenderer.ts renderStep contains the in-place redraw escape `\x1b[1A` and `\x1b[2K` guarded by an isTTY check; non-TTY path emits only the terminal status line

[UI-observable] running `fabric install` on an already-up-to-date repo (even when a store prompt fired mid-pipeline) shows a SINGLE health card titled '已是最新/up to date' with NO '3 installed' detail and NO per-stage replay
[UI-observable] running `fabric install` in a TTY shows each stage as ONE line (running spinner overwritten by ✓), not a running line + a success line stacked

pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test exits 0 (locale parity) && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test exits 0 (pipeline collapse + reinstall-misreport tests updated) && pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit exits 0
convergence.verification: Inspect pipeline.ts buildSummary + collapse gate; run the existing pipeline collapse/idempotent tests (search packages/cli for *pipeline* / *collapse* / *health* specs) and add a case where buffer.flushed=true but allIdempotent → still collapses; run a non-TTY install to confirm single-line stages.
convergence.definition_of_done: A pure re-install (all idempotent) always collapses to the health card and never reports 'N installed'; a stage that did change still reports its installed count; the per-stage running line is replaced in place on TTY and omitted on non-TTY, eliminating the double line.

files.change: buildSummary ran-stage value branches on r.changed (uptodate vs installed-count via t()). Collapse gate fires on `buffer !== undefined && allIdempotent(context)` (drop `!buffer.flushed`), guarding the normal-path flush so a flushed buffer is not double-emitted. Per-step running→success detail also keyed off changed where it currently uses installed.length.

On TTY+color, when the same step transitions running→terminal, write `\x1b[1A\x1b[2K` before the final buildStepLine to overwrite the running line. On non-TTY, skip emitting the 'running' line and print only the terminal line.
Add cli.install.stage.uptodate='up to date' and cli.install.stage.installed-count='{count} installed'.
Add the SAME keys: 已最新 / {count} 项已安装.

implementation: Add the two stage keys to both locale tables.
Rewrite buildSummary's ran-stage value to branch on r.changed via t().
Loosen the collapse gate to fire on all-idempotent even after a prompt-flushed buffer; guard the normal-path flush against double-emit.
Implement single-line spinner redraw in renderStep (TTY: \x1b[1A\x1b[2K; non-TTY: terminal-only).
Update/extend pipeline collapse + reinstall-misreport tests, then run shared+cli tests + tsc.

success_metrics: all-idempotent re-install collapses even after a prompt flush

reference.pattern: allIdempotent already keys off r.changed (pipeline.ts ~line 373) — mirror that into the detail row and the collapse gate; buildHealthCheckSummary is the existing collapse card

rationale.chosen_approach: Make the detail row and collapse gate consistent with allIdempotent's existing r.changed truth source; redraw the spinner in place rather than buffering two lines.
decision_factors: allIdempotent already uses r.changed — installed.length is the lone inconsistency
buffer.flushed is set by a legitimate prompt, not by an actual change
rationale.tradeoffs: In-place redraw assumes the running line was the immediately previous stdout write; renderStep already serializes per-step so this holds.

risks: Removing `!buffer.flushed` from the gate could double-emit if the normal-path flush still runs — the flush guard (buffer.flushed early-return in flushBuffer) must cover the collapse branch.
`\x1b[1A\x1b[2K` on a non-TTY would corrupt logs — strictly gate behind process.stdout.isTTY.
